items.title: Validate: RAM region 0002 behaves like counter
items.description: Address $0002 is updated with INC/DEC/shift-style read-modify-write instructions. That pattern often indicates a counter, timer, or packed state byte.

evidence.title: RAM hypothesis counter
evidence.excerpt: Address $0002 is updated with INC/DEC/shift-style read-modify-write instructions. That pattern often indicates a counter, timer, or packed state byte.

items.title: Validate: RAM region 0002 behaves like pointer_pair
items.description: Zero-page pointer $0002-$0003 is assembled by consecutive load/store pairs. Pointer bytes are loaded dynamically, so the final target depends on runtime state. Pointer setup comes from a probable code island and should be validated in context.

evidence.title: RAM hypothesis pointer_pair
evidence.excerpt: Zero-page pointer $0002-$0003 is assembled by consecutive load/store pairs. Pointer bytes are loaded dynamically, so the final target depends on runtime state. Pointer setup comes from a probable code island and should be validated in context.

items.title: Validate: RAM region 0003 behaves like flag
items.description: Address $0003 is written with a small immediate set () and later read back. That pattern is more consistent with a flag/mode byte than with arbitrary data.

evidence.title: RAM hypothesis flag
evidence.excerpt: Address $0003 is written with a small immediate set () and later read back. That pattern is more consistent with a flag/mode byte than with arbitrary data.

items.title: Validate: RAM region 000B behaves like table
items.description: Indexed or loop-based accesses cluster around $000B, $FD47. 2 consecutive absolute-indexed mixed access(es) use Y as the table index. Referenced bases cluster around $000B, $FD47.

evidence.title: RAM hypothesis table
evidence.excerpt: Indexed or loop-based accesses cluster around $000B, $FD47. 2 consecutive absolute-indexed mixed access(es) use Y as the table index. Referenced bases cluster around $000B, $FD47.

items.title: Validate: Segment 3D72 classified as music_data
items.description: SID driver at $3CF0 reads data via indexed addressing from $3D72. Data at this address is fed to SID registers and likely contains music or SFX sequences.

evidence.title: Classification for $3D72-$4134
evidence.excerpt: SID driver at $3CF0 reads data via indexed addressing from $3D72. Data at this address is fed to SID registers and likely contains music or SFX sequences.

items.title: Validate: Segment 5469 classified as charset
items.description: Region length is 49 x 8 bytes, which matches glyph storage. 47% of glyphs have plausible 8x8 structure. 27% of glyphs have empty top or bottom rows, common in character sets.

evidence.title: Classification for $5469-$5470
evidence.excerpt: Region length is 49 x 8 bytes, which matches glyph storage. 47% of glyphs have plausible 8x8 structure. 27% of glyphs have empty top or bottom rows, common in character sets.

items.title: Validate: Segment 8FA2 classified as sprite
items.description: Length is 1 x 64 bytes, matching C64 sprite storage. Rendered blocks show non-empty 24x21 pixel silhouettes in $8FA2-$8FE1. Row density and transition metrics are closer to sprite shapes than random noise.

evidence.title: Classification for $8FA2-$8FE1
evidence.excerpt: Length is 1 x 64 bytes, matching C64 sprite storage. Rendered blocks show non-empty 24x21 pixel silhouettes in $8FA2-$8FE1. Row density and transition metrics are closer to sprite shapes than random noise.

items.title: When does disk level loading begin?
items.description: The current static evidence does not isolate the exact hand-off from intro bootstrap to level payload loading.
